title: Web Dashboard
problem: Teams need a shared web interface to browse, search, and edit feature provenance records — with role-appropriate views for engineers, product managers, support staff, and end users — that stays in sync with the local filesystem.
analysis: Next.js 15 app with tRPC v11 and Drizzle ORM on Postgres. The LAC_LOCAL_DIR watcher syncs feature.json files to the DB within 300ms of any change. Four RBAC dashboard views (Dev/PM/Support/User) show different columns for different audiences. The wizard guides step-by-step feature completion. As of iteration 3: lineage tab, annotation icons, empty-state component, relative-time display, accessibility improvements (aria-labels), copy button with checkmark feedback.
implementation: Routes: /features (list with debounced search, status filter pills, group-by-status), /features/[id] (detail with 6 tabs: overview/guide/lineage/decisions/annotations/history), /features/[id]/wizard, /features/[id]/json (raw editor), /dashboard (completeness table with RBAC views, SVG histogram). tRPC procedures: listFeatures, getFeature, updateFeature, addAnnotation, exportFeatureJson, getLineage, getFeatureByKey. File watcher: chokidar on LAC_LOCAL_DIR, upserts to DB on change. Global keyboard shortcuts: /, n, g d, g l. EmptyState component for empty lists.

decisions.decision: tRPC over REST for the webapp API layer
decisions.rationale: End-to-end type safety between server procedures and React Query hooks. No manual type duplication.

decisions.decision: RBAC views via URL param (?view=dev|pm|support|user) rather than user auth roles
decisions.rationale: Simpler. No auth needed for local-first use. Anyone can switch views. Shareable URLs.

decisions.decision: Tabs as URL hash fragments rather than separate routes
decisions.rationale: Preserves scroll position and bookmarkability. No server round-trip needed to switch tabs.
alternativesConsidered: Separate routes per tab — requires server round-trip, loses scroll position

knownLimitations: No multi-tenant auth yet — single org, local-only
Lineage graph is text only — no visual tree
Audit log exists in DB but not rendered in UI yet
Next.js API bundle may have issues in some build environments (pre-existing)
